name: linuxcnc-lsp
description: Language support for LinuxCNC HAL, INI and G-code files

languages.comment: No .ini extension binding on purpose: a LinuxCNC machine INI is detected by content (see linuxcnc.iniDetection) so we do not hijack unrelated .ini files.

linuxcnc.libDir.markdownDescription: Path to the LinuxCNC system HAL library directory used to resolve `LIB:` HALFILE references. Leave empty if LinuxCNC is not installed locally; `LIB:` files are then treated as external and dependent checks are disabled.

linuxcnc.metadata.path.markdownDescription: Override path to a metadata JSON database (regenerated from a specific LinuxCNC version). Empty uses the bundled database.

linuxcnc.activeMachine.markdownDescription: When a `.hal` file is shared by more than one machine (`.ini`), pin which machine provides its context — diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition/references, and completion all use the pinned machine. Set to the INI's file name or path, e.g. `MachineA.ini` or `configs/a/MachineA.ini`. Empty = use the first machine found. Tip: run **LinuxCNC: Select Active Machine** to pick from a list.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Treat a `.ini` file as a LinuxCNC machine INI only when its content has machine sections (e.g. `[EMC]`, `[HAL]`, `[KINS]`, `[TRAJ]`) — does not hijack unrelated `.ini` files.
Treat every `.ini` file as a LinuxCNC machine INI.
Never auto-assign the LinuxCNC INI language; assign it manually via the language picker.
linuxcnc.iniDetection.markdownDescription: How `.ini` files are recognized as LinuxCNC machine configs. `.hal` and `.ngc` are always recognized by extension.

linuxcnc.trace.server.description: Trace the communication between VSCode and the language server.
